title: elymus arenarius var. scabrinervis
intro: Elymus arenarius var. scabrinervis, also known as beach wildrye, is a variety of the Elymus arenarius plant, a type of grass native to coastal areas of Europe and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin from irritation and infection. The health benefits of beach wildrye include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as potentially aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Beach wildrye can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
